description:
You are acting as an in-house configuration-management assistant.  
A fictitious daemon called “dummy_service” ships its user-editable configuration in  

/home/user/configs/dummy_service.conf  

Current contents of the file:

#-----------------------------------
# Dummy Service configuration file
# Maintainer: DevOps Team
MAX_CLIENTS=50
#-----------------------------------

Task:  
1. Create a one-shot backup of the current configuration before changing anything.  
   • The backup must live in the same directory and be named **dummy_service.conf.bak**.  
   • Its contents must be an exact byte-for-byte copy of the original file.

2. Apply the following configuration changes to /home/user/configs/dummy_service.conf:  
   a) Change the existing parameter MAX_CLIENTS so its value becomes **100**.  
   b) Append a brand-new parameter line **LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG** immediately after MAX_CLIENTS.  
   c) Leave every other line in the file untouched and keep overall file order the same (except for the new line right after MAX_CLIENTS).

   The final file **must look exactly like this** (including comments, hashes, blank lines and delimiter line):
   #-----------------------------------
   # Dummy Service configuration file
   # Maintainer: DevOps Team
   MAX_CLIENTS=100
   LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG
   #-----------------------------------

3. Record the change in a simple CSV-style audit log that a higher-level configuration-manager later picks up.  
   • The log lives at **/home/user/configs/change_log.csv**.  
   • If the file does not yet exist, create it; if it exists, just append the new lines.  
   • Use this exact column order, separated by a single literal pipe character (|) and one space on each side of the pipe:
     TIMESTAMP | USER | FILE | FIELD | OLD_VALUE | NEW_VALUE
   • TIMESTAMP must be the current local time generated by the command date "+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S".  
   • USER must always be “user”.  
   • FILE must always be “dummy_service.conf”.  
   • FIELD, OLD_VALUE and NEW_VALUE reflect the concrete change inside the file.

   Therefore two new lines have to be appended, one for each change:

   YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS | user | dummy_service.conf | MAX_CLIENTS | 50 | 100  
   YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS | user | dummy_service.conf | LOG_LEVEL | (absent) | DEBUG  

   Replace YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS with the actual timestamp you obtain at runtime.  
   For the LOG_LEVEL line, the literal text “(absent)” is required in the OLD_VALUE column because that key did not exist before.

Verification details the test suite will check:
• /home/user/configs/dummy_service.conf.bak exists and is a byte-for-byte copy of the original pre-change file.  
• /home/user/configs/dummy_service.conf exists and matches exactly the four-line snippet shown above.  
• /home/user/configs/change_log.csv contains two new lines whose non-timestamp fields exactly match the specification.  
• The two timestamp fields must have identical values down to the second.

You should be able to accomplish everything with a simple sequence of 3–4 standard shell commands (cp, sed/echo, date, etc.).  
Do not show me the commands; just run them in the terminal to achieve the described state.
truth:
Initial state before the agent begins:

1. Directory /home/user/configs already exists and is writable by the current user.
2. /home/user/configs/dummy_service.conf contains exactly:
#-----------------------------------
# Dummy Service configuration file
# Maintainer: DevOps Team
MAX_CLIENTS=50
#-----------------------------------

3. /home/user/configs/change_log.csv does NOT exist.

Required final state after successful completion:

A. File /home/user/configs/dummy_service.conf.bak
   • Permissions: 0644
   • Contents (5 lines):
#-----------------------------------
# Dummy Service configuration file
# Maintainer: DevOps Team
MAX_CLIENTS=50
#-----------------------------------

B. File /home/user/configs/dummy_service.conf
   • Permissions: 0644
   • Contents (6 lines, including the newly inserted one):
#-----------------------------------
# Dummy Service configuration file
# Maintainer: DevOps Team
MAX_CLIENTS=100
LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG
#-----------------------------------

C. File /home/user/configs/change_log.csv
   • Permissions: 0644
   • The file ends with two new lines (add \n at EOL). The lines must have identical timestamps.
   • Format exactly:
{TS} | user | dummy_service.conf | MAX_CLIENTS | 50 | 100
{TS} | user | dummy_service.conf | LOG_LEVEL | (absent) | DEBUG

   where {TS} is the same ISO-like timestamp produced by date "+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S".

The automated grader will:
1. Compute sha256sum of *.bak and ensure it matches the pre-change config.
2. Verify dummy_service.conf matches the exact five-line “after” text.
3. Parse change_log.csv, capture the last two lines, ensure pipe-separated columns match the required literal strings and that the timestamp columns are identical and valid (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS).
